_comment: Hand-labelled queries for the TF-IDF vs BM25 evaluation harness. Every URL listed under relevant_urls was sampled directly from data/index.json and verified to be in the candidate set produced by the project's existing find() logic, so any difference between the rankers can only come from how the candidates are *ordered*, not which candidates are returned.

relevant_urls.justification: Tag landing page that aggregates every quote on the site explicitly tagged with 'love' — by construction the most relevant page for the bare query 'love'.
Second page of the 'love' tag listing — same canonical match as page 1 of the same tag.
Pablo Neruda's author page surfaces because his sole quoted line on the site is explicitly about love.
Bob Marley's author page contains the much-quoted '...the right one' line about love, making it a high-relevance hit.

queries.notes: Tests how each ranker handles a very common term: 156 documents contain 'love'. The discriminator should be IDF / length normalisation, not retrieval.

relevant_urls.justification: Tag landing page for 'life' quotes — by design the canonical hit for the bare query 'life'.
Second page of the 'life' tag listing — equivalently relevant.
Einstein's author page hosts his 'There are only two ways to live your life' quote, an iconic life-themed entry.

queries.notes: Another high-frequency term (178 docs). 'life' co-occurs with site boilerplate, so length normalisation should differentiate the two rankers.

relevant_urls.justification: Tag landing page for 'good' quotes — the bare query 'good' should rank this first.

queries.notes: Mid-frequency term (30 docs). Smaller candidate pool means ranking differences between TF-IDF and BM25 are easier to inspect by eye.

relevant_urls.justification: Tag page that exists *because* Tolkien's 'Not all those who wander are lost' is tagged 'journey' — the canonical match for the conjunction.
Tolkien's wander/journey quote is also tagged 'bilbo'; this page therefore contains both terms in the exact context the user is asking about.
Same Tolkien quote about journeys/quests, surfaced via the 'quest' tag — strong topical relevance.

queries.notes: AND-semantics: 7 documents contain both terms. Tag pages should rank above the page/7 listing because their token totals are smaller (length-normalisation favours them).

relevant_urls.justification: The 'imagination' tag exists for Einstein's 'Imagination is more important than knowledge' quote — exactly the page a user asking about Einstein and imagination wants.

queries.notes: Tight 3-document candidate set. Ranker quality is judged by whether the tag page is ranked above the two general listing pages.

relevant_urls.justification: Tag page collecting every Austen-attributed quote on the site — directly answers the conjunction 'austen books'.
Books tag page — Austen's library quote is filed here, so the page co-mentions both terms in a tightly relevant way.
Aliteracy tag carries an Austen quote about not reading good books, satisfying both terms.
'Classic' tag hosts Austen's 'classic — a book which people praise and don't read' quote, which mentions books explicitly.

queries.notes: Larger candidate set (17 docs); tests how each ranker copes with boilerplate dilution from sidebar tag links.

relevant_urls.justification: Love-tag landing page contains multiple quotes opening with 'love is...' (e.g. 'Love is that condition in which the happiness of another person is essential').
Second page of the love tag also carries quotes phrased 'love is...' — equally on-topic.

queries.notes: Quoted phrase forces consecutive-position matching. The candidate set (12 docs) is filtered before ranking, so we are evaluating ordering quality, not phrase detection.

relevant_urls.justification: Friendship tag includes the 'half a truth is often a great lie' line, where 'the truth' appears as a contiguous phrase in the tagline context.
Life tag page 2 carries the 'the truth' phrase in the context of an Einstein-style life-philosophy quote.

queries.notes: Tests phrase ranking on a 7-document candidate set; deeper documents like the homepage should be down-ranked relative to focused tag pages.

relevant_urls.justification: Tag page literally titled 'bilbo' — unambiguous canonical relevance for a bare 'bilbo' query.
Tolkien's wander quote about Bilbo lives under the 'journey' tag too, making this page relevant for any reader looking for the source.

queries.notes: Rare term (7 docs). High IDF means BM25 and TF-IDF should converge on the same ordering; primary risk is length normalisation behaving badly on sparse documents.

relevant_urls.justification: Pablo Neruda's dedicated author page — the obvious canonical answer for a one-word author-name query.

queries.notes: Very rare term (4 docs). Tests that high-IDF terms still place author pages first instead of generic tag pages that merely link to the author.
quotes.toscrape.com hosts no Marx-attributed quotes; the index has zero postings for 'marx'. Both rankers should return the empty list, which both P@5 and MRR conventionally treat as 0.
